gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer found the initial command whitelist rejected generated planner records because commands include shell; the fix allows shell and adds planner-to-executor regression coverage.
verification: python -m pytest tests/test_benchmark_plan_executor.py; python -m pytest tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_release_bundle.py; python -m pytest -q; poetry check && poetry install --dry-run && poetry install --dry-run --extras databricks --extras test && poetry build; GPT-5.5 review finding about generated command shell field resolved
what_changed: Benchmark plan executor now rejects unsupported top-level benchmark plan keys and unsupported per-command keys before running commands while accepting generated command shell renderings.
why: Keeps benchmark job plans fail-closed and prevents ad hoc/debug fields from silently influencing release execution provenance without breaking generated planner records.
